https://research.colfax-intl.com/categor.title: Categorical Foundations for CuTe Layouts
https://research.colfax-intl.com/categor.excerpt: In GPU programming, performance depends critically on how data is stored and accessed in memory. While the data we care about is typically multi-dimensional, the GPU’s memory is fundamentally one-dimensional. This means that when we want to load, store, or otherwise manipulate data, we need to map its multi-dimensional logical coordinates to one-dimensional physical coordinates. This mapping, known as alayout, is essential for reading from and writing to memory correctly and efficiently. Moreove

This post was authored in conjunction with members of the CUTLASS team.

https://research.colfax-intl.com/cutlass.excerpt: No series of CUDA® tutorials is complete without a section on GEMM (GEneral Matrix Multiplication). https://research.colfax-intl.com/a-note-.title: A note on the algebra of CuTe Layouts
https://research.colfax-intl.com/a-note-.excerpt: The core abstraction of NVIDIA’s CUTLASS library for high-performance linear algebra is the CuTe Layout. In this technical note, we give a rigorous, mathematical treatment of the algebra of these layouts and certain layout operations. Currently, the main goal is to lay down conditions for when the operations of complementation, composition, and logical division are well-defined, which may be of general use to CUTLASS developers. This note should be read as complementary to the discussion of thes

https://research.colfax-intl.com/nvidia-.title: A Case Study in CUDA Kernel Fusion: Implementing FlashAttention-2 on NVIDIA Hopper Architecture using the CUTLASS Library
https://research.colfax-intl.com/nvidia-.excerpt: We provide an optimized implementation of the forward pass of FlashAttention-2, a popular memory-aware scaled dot-product attention algorithm, as a custom fused CUDA®kernel targeting NVIDIA Hopper™architecture and written using the open-source CUTLASS library. In doing so, we explain the challenges and techniques involved in fusing online-softmax with back-to-back GEMM kernels, utilizing the Hopper-specific Tensor Memory Accelerator (TMA) and Warpgroup Matrix-Multiply-Accumulate (WGMMA) instruct

https://research.colfax-intl.com/nvidia-.title: Developing CUDA Kernels for GEMM on NVIDIA Hopper Architecture using CUTLASS
https://research.colfax-intl.com/nvidia-.excerpt: We explain how to develop NVIDIA CUDA®kernels for optimized general matrix multiplication (GEMM) on NVIDIA Hopper™architecture using the template collection CUTLASS and its core library CuTe. Our main contribution is to provide an implementation of a GEMM kernel that uses the Tensor Memory Accelerator (TMA) and Warp Group Matrix-Multiply-Accumulate (WGMMA) operations introduced with NVIDIA Hopper™architecture.
